title: Sample-efficient SuperMarioBros-NES Level1-2 completion

objective.primary_metric_note: Primary metric to maximize during training. For this single-source Level1-2 goal it should match train/info/level_complete/from/0-1/rate once the source window is full; out-of-process eval remains the promotion check.
objective.attempt_denominator: Source-level Level1-2 attempts. In terminal-on-level-change runs this is equivalent to completed terminal episodes; in continued multi-level runs it also counts non-terminal natural level transitions.
forbidden_stop_rules: Do not use rollout-count completion stops because their meaning changes with n_envs, n_steps, and rollout cadence.

confirmation_tie_breakers: higher success count across confirmation seeds
higher completion rate at fixed budgets
higher external checkpoint eval completion rate

runtime.runtime_image_ref: digest-pinned GHCR train image from rlab-train-image.json

seed_protocol.same_seed_note: Same seed is a controlled randomization label, not bitwise reproducibility under default CUDA.

cap_policy.ratchet_rule: If a confirmed baseline succeeds on all confirmation seeds below the active cap, lower future caps to the slowest successful seed rounded up to the next 100k.
cap_policy.increase_rule: Do not increase the cap unless the goal changes or an infrastructure bug invalidates the baseline.

search_protocol.screen: Run screen seeds first. Reject obvious negatives. Advance candidates that reach the success window, materially improve fixed-budget completion metrics, or look promising enough to justify confirmation.
search_protocol.confirm: Promote only if success count is at least as good as the incumbent, median time_to_100_of_100 is better, there is no severe reliability regression, and all runs either stop by the success window or reach the cap.
search_protocol.baseline_definition: A baseline is the best confirmed config, not the best single run. Single lucky runs generate hypotheses; they do not replace the baseline.

incumbent.status: single-seed teacher evidence; not a confirmed baseline; revalidate active launches under post21 runtime

default_train_profile_note: Queue-backed launches are profileless by default; use the spec run_target plus a digest-pinned runtime image.

execution.manual_trainers: Avoid ad hoc second trainers through sky exec, docker exec, or manual host shells for comparable runs.
execution.backfill_rule: Backfill finished slots only while the next queued run still has decision value.

determinism.default: Measure the real training distribution. Do not enable deterministic CUDA flags by default because they can slow training and alter the optimizer/kernel path.

debug_only.note: If used, mark deterministic CUDA as a separate runtime condition.

historical_context: Level1-2 was previously useful as a single-level teacher for Level1-1/Level1-2 distillation, but that is not the same as a confirmed five-seed baseline.
The old completed-episode stop flags are retired from new launches; use attempt-window metrics from configured info events instead.
Historical BASELINE names are retired. In this repo, baseline means a config confirmed under the current 100/100 protocol.
notes: Use this file as the operator-facing current contract; goal-owned specs and recipe evidence live beside it under this goal directory.
Do not promote a policy from final checkpoint reward alone.
Prefer paired confirmation against the incumbent before declaring a new baseline.
